perturbation.original_text: The term "Evaluation Material" also shall be deemed to include all notes, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations or other documents (whether in paper, electronic or any other form) prepared by each or its Representatives which contain, reflect or are based upon, in whole or in part, the information furnished to such party or its Representatives pursuant hereto which is not available to the general public and would otherwise constitute Evaluation Material hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: The term "Evaluation Material" shall include only final reports and summaries that are clearly marked as such by the disclosing party. Preliminary drafts, working papers, and oral communications do not constitute Evaluation Material.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by limiting what constitutes 'Evaluation Material' to only final reports and summaries clearly marked as such, excluding preliminary drafts, working papers, and oral communications. This directly conflicts with the original definition which includes 'all notes, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations or other documents'. This creates uncertainty about which documents are subject to the confidentiality obligations. This contradiction is in Section 3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party hereby agrees that it and its Representatives shall use the other's Evaluation Material solely for the purposes of evaluating a possible Transaction between the parties and, if the parties determine to proceed with a Transaction, the negotiation, execution and performance of a definitive agreement with respect thereto, and that the disclosing party's Evaluation Material will be kept confidential and each party and its Representatives will not disclose or use for purposes other than the purposes stated above any of the other's Evaluation Material in any other manner whatsoever; provided, however, that (i) the receiving party may make any disclosure of such information to which the disclosing party gives its prior written consent and (ii) any of such information may be disclosed to the receiving party's Representatives which need to know such information for the sole purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction between the parties, who are informed of the confidential nature of such information and are subject to confidentiality and non-use obligations at least as restrictive as defined herein. Each party is aware of the restrictions imposed by the United States securities laws on the purchase or sale of securities by any person who have received material, nonpublic information from the issuer of such securities and on the communication of such information to any other person when it is reasonably foreseeable that such other person is likely to purchase or sell such securities in reliance upon such information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of 'Evaluation Material' to only final reports and summaries. This directly contradicts the broader definition in the original text and the contradicted text, which includes 'all notes, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations or other documents'. The contradicted text states that each party will keep the other's Evaluation Material confidential, but because of the new definition of evaluation material, it is unclear whether preliminary drafts, working papers, and oral communications are subject to confidentiality, creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that a party or its Representatives are requested or required (by oral questions, interrogatories, requests for information or documents in legal proceedings, subpoena, civil investigative demand or other similar process) to disclose any of the other party's Evaluation Material, the party requested or required to make the disclosure shall provide the other party with prompt notice of any such request or requirement so that the other party may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy and/or waive compliance with the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that a party or its Representatives are requested or required to disclose any of the other party's Evaluation Material, the disclosing party has the sole discretion to decide whether or not to provide notice and allow the receiving party to seek a protective order.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by giving the disclosing party the sole discretion on whether to provide notice in the event of a required disclosure. This contradicts the original text which states that the receiving party 'shall provide the other party with prompt notice'. This creates uncertainty as the receiving party may not be aware of a required disclosure. This contradiction is in Section 5.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that a party or its Representatives are requested or required (by oral questions, interrogatories, requests for information or documents in legal proceedings, subpoena, civil investigative demand or other similar process) to disclose any of the other party's Evaluation Material, the party requested or required to make the disclosure shall provide the other party with prompt notice of any such request or requirement so that the other party may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy and/or waive compliance with the provisions of this Agreement. If, in the absence of a protective order or other remedy or the receipt of a waiver by such other party, the party requested or required to make the disclosure or any of its Representatives are nonetheless, in the opinion of counsel, legally compelled to disclose the other party's Evaluation Material to any tribunal, the party requested or required to make the disclosure or its Representative may, without liability hereunder, disclose to such tribunal only that portion of the other party's Evaluation Material which such counsel advises is legally required to be disclosed, provided that the party requested or required to make the disclosure exercises its reasonable efforts to preserve the confidentiality of the other party's Evaluation Material, including, without limitation, by reasonably cooperating with the other party to obtain an appropriate protective order or other reliable assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded the other party's Evaluation Material by such tribunal.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original text, giving the disclosing party sole discretion in providing notice of a required disclosure. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which mandates that the party 'shall provide the other party with prompt notice'. This alteration creates a clear inconsistency, as one section requires notice while the other allows the disclosing party to decide, leading to uncertainty and potential unenforceability as outlined in the Perturbation Explanation and fitting the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the return or distruction of the Evaluation Material, each party and its Representatives will continue to be bound by its obligations of confidentiality and other obligations hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the return or destruction of the Evaluation Material, all obligations of confidentiality and other obligations hereunder shall immediately cease.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction about when the confidentiality obligations cease. The original text states that obligations continue even after return or destruction, while the modified text states they cease. This creates confusion about the duration of confidentiality. This contradiction is in Section 7.
perturbation.contradicted_location: (9) Duration of Confidentiality and Non-use Obligation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties agree that the obligations pertaining to confidentiality and use of the Evaluation Material set forth in sections (2) through (6) above, and this clause (9), shall survive the expiration or termination of this Agreement until the expiration of the period ending five (5) years after the date first set forth above.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that confidentiality obligations continue even after the return or destruction of Evaluation Material. The changed text states these obligations cease upon return or destruction. This directly contradicts the contradicted text (clause 9), which states that confidentiality obligations survive termination of the agreement for five years. The change introduces a clear and impactful contradiction regarding the duration of confidentiality, making the terms inconsistent and potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees that neither party nor any of its Representatives shall have any liability to the other party or to any of its Representatives relating to or resulting from the use of or reliance upon such other party's Evaluation Material or any errors therein or omissions therefrom. Only those representations or warranties which are made in a final definitive agreement regarding the Transaction, when, as and if executed, and subject to such limitations and restrictions as may be specified therein, will have any legal effect.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party acknowledges that while best efforts are made, the disclosing party may be held liable for significant errors or omissions in the Evaluation Material that directly lead to financial damages for the receiving party.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding liability for errors or omissions in the Evaluation Material. The original text states that neither party has liability, while the modified text states that the disclosing party may be held liable. This creates uncertainty about potential legal recourse. This contradiction is in Section 8.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party understands and acknowledges that neither party nor any of its Representatives makes any representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Material make available by it or to it. Each party agrees that neither party nor any of its Representatives shall have any liability to the other party or to any of its Representatives relating to or resulting from the use of or reliance upon such other party's Evaluation Material or any errors therein or omissions therefrom. Only those representations or warranties which are made in a final definitive agreement regarding the Transaction, when, as and if executed, and subject to such limitations and restrictions as may be specified therein, will have any legal effect.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that neither party has liability for errors or omissions in the Evaluation Material. The changed text introduces a contradiction by stating that the disclosing party may be held liable for significant errors or omissions in the Evaluation Material. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which also states that neither party has any liability for errors or omissions in the Evaluation Material. This creates a clear inconsistency regarding liability, leading to a strong in-text contradiction, as defined by the 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: Any dispute between the parties arising out of or connected to this Agreement or its enforceability or interpretation, including any injunctive relief, shall be brought and resolved solely in the federal or state courts located within the Southern District of New York, and the parties hereby submit to the personal jurisdiction of said courts.
perturbation.changed_text: Any dispute arising under this agreement may be settled by binding arbitration in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association, and such arbitration shall be the exclusive remedy for any such dispute.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction about the method of dispute resolution. The original text mandates resolution in the courts of the Southern District of New York, while the modified text mandates binding arbitration. This creates uncertainty about the proper forum for resolving disputes. This contradiction is in Section 15.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be governed by an construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York applicable to agreements made and to be performed within such State. Any dispute between the parties arising out of or connected to this Agreement or its enforceability or interpretation, including any injunctive relief, shall be brought and resolved solely in the federal or state courts located within the Southern District of New York, and the parties hereby submit to the personal jurisdiction of said courts.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that disputes will be resolved in the federal or state courts within the Southern District of New York. The changed text mandates binding arbitration as the exclusive remedy for any dispute. This directly contradicts the original text, creating a strong in-text contradiction about the method of dispute resolution, as described in the perturbation explanation.

perturbation.original_text: During the term of this Agreement, neither party shall, without the prior written consent of the other party, directly or indirectly solicit the employment of any employee of such other party, unless such employee seeks employment on an unsolicited basis or in response to general solicitations or advertising or third party employment agencies, provided that such general solicitations or advertising is made, taken out and distributed in the ordinary course and not in an effort to specifically target employees of the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party may solicit the employment of employees from the other party, as long as they provide a 30-day written notice to the other party before making an offer to such employee.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by allowing employee solicitation with a 30-day notice, while the original text prohibits it without prior written consent. This creates uncertainty about the permissible methods of employee recruitment. This contradiction is in Section 10.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the term of this Agreement, neither party shall, without the prior written consent of the other party, directly or indirectly solicit the employment of any employee of such other party, unless such employee seeks employment on an unsolicited basis or in response to general solicitations or advertising or third party employment agencies, provided that such general solicitations or advertising is made, taken out and distributed in the ordinary course and not in an effort to specifically target employees of the other party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original and contradicted text. The original text states that neither party shall solicit employment of the other party's employees without prior written consent, while the changed text allows solicitation with only a 30-day written notice. This creates a direct conflict in the permissible methods of employee recruitment. The explanation provided states that the change introduces a contradiction, and it aligns with the 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' definition, because key obligations are defined inconsistently.
